title: NITI Aayog moots  ₹50,000-crore fund, schemes for biotech boost
content:
• NITI Aayog has proposed a ₹50,000-crore BioEconomy Growth Fund for 2026-2035, along with six national biotechnology missions, to build a $691-billion bioeconomy by 2035 and generate over 30 million high-value jobs.
• India's bioeconomy expanded from $10 billion in 2014 to $195.3 billion in 2025, now contributing 4.8% to the country's GDP, according to the India BioEconomy Report 2026.
• The 'Roadmap for Building India as a Leading BioEconomy Powerhouse by 2035' by NITI Aayog recommends establishing a National BioData Council, a Bio-IP Accelerated Pathway, and calls for faster regulatory approvals for emerging technologies like cell and gene therapies.

title: Taylor Farms lettuce supplied to Taco Bell emerges as potential source of US cyclosporiasis outbreak: Report
content:
• Taylor Farms, a supplier of shredded iceberg lettuce to Yum Brands' Taco Bell restaurants, has been identified as a potential source of contamination in a cyclosporiasis outbreak.
• The cyclosporiasis outbreak has caused over 4,300 reported cases and at least 100 hospitalizations in southeastern Michigan.
• The CDC identified a possible link among cases from four states (Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and West Virginia), with cases detected in at least 34 states and confirmed illnesses expected to rise till the end of August.

title: Iran asks Houthis to prepare Red Sea blockade if US targets power infra: Report
content:
• The current conflict began on February 28, when Israel and the United States launched attacks on Iran, prompting Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz.
• The Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime chokepoint, previously handled around one-fifth of global energy supplies.
• The Mandeb Strait, which connects the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden, is a strategic waterway where Houthi forces are now awaiting orders to begin blockade operations.

title: RBI rejects lenders’ bid to acquire pre-NPA assets, resell to same borrowers
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) rejected lenders' requests to acquire specified non-financial assets (SNFA) before a borrower turns non-performing (pre-NPA) and to sell such assets back to the same borrower or its related parties.
• Specified Non-Financial Assets (SNFA) can be acquired only when a regulated entity's exposure to a borrower is classified as non-performing, and banks must dispose of such assets within seven years.
• The RBI rejected allowing SNFA acquisition for Special Mention Account (SMA) categories, with SMA-0 defined as repayment overdue between 1-30 days, SMA-1 between 31-60 days, and SMA-2 between 61-90 days.

title: Watch: Zohran Mamdani joins Rikers Island inmates to watch FIFA world cup semifinal
content:
• Mayor Zohran Mamdani joined inmates at Rikers Island jail complex to watch the FIFA World Cup semifinal.
• The World Cup screenings were part of a program launched to reward inmates who had remained free of disciplinary incidents for at least 30 days and demonstrated good behavior, with approximately 4,500 out of 6,600 inmates participating in nearly 90 screenings.
• Mayor Mamdani has pledged to honor the 2019 New York City law mandating the closure of Rikers Island, although he acknowledged the 2027 deadline is unlikely to be met.

title: Mahadev betting case: ED records TV host Shefali Bagga's statement
content:
• The Enforcement Directorate (ED) recorded TV host Shefali Bagga's statement under the provisions of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) in the Mahadev online betting app case.
• The alleged proceeds of crime generated through the Mahadev online betting app racket amount to ₹6,000 crore.
• The ED has named 74 entities as accused across five chargesheets filed before a special PMLA court in Raipur as part of its ongoing investigation into the Mahadev online betting app case.

title: Trump bought shares in over 20 companies, then promoted them to millions on Truth Social: Report
content:
• On 15 April 2025, Donald Trump posted about NVIDIA's $500 billion commitment to build AI supercomputers in the US, days after reportedly investing between $200,000 and $500,000 in NVIDIA stock.
• Through May, Donald Trump sold Micron Technology shares worth between $90,000 and $3.7 million, while simultaneously buying between $246,000 and $3.7 million in NVIDIA stock.
• On 23 July 2025, Donald Trump bought between $500,000 and $1 million in Tesla stock, and unlike many previous presidents, he has not placed his assets in a blind trust.

title: Govt to replace WPI with Producer Price Index for future procurement contracts
content:
• The government will replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with the Producer Price Index (PPI) for future public procurement contracts.
• The Department of Expenditure advised ministries to use the Producer Price Index (PPI) in place of the WPI for future contracts in an email communication dated July 10.
• India introduced a new Producer Price Index (PPI) alongside a revised WPI series on June 15, as part of an overhaul of inflation measurement.

title: India-EU trade pact to be signed by end of this year: Piyush Goyal
content:
• India and the 27-nation EU will sign the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) by the end of 2026, with it coming into effect in the first quarter of 2027 (February-March).
• Under the India-EU FTA, 93 per cent of Indian shipments will enjoy duty-free access to the 27-nation European Union bloc.
• India has announced Rs 1.27 lakh crore for the second edition of the India Semiconductor Mission.

title: RBI's financial inclusion index rises 4.48 pc in FY26
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Financial Inclusion Index (FI-Index) rose by 4.48% to 70.0 in March 2026, up from 67.0 in March 2025.
• The RBI's composite Financial Inclusion Index (FI-Index) was first published in August 2021 for the fiscal year ending March 2021.
• The FI-Index has three sub-indices: Access (35%), Usage (45%), and Quality (20%).
